segments.text: For the choir director; with stringed instruments.
Answer me when I call, O God of my righteousness! You have given me relief when I was in distress. Be gracious to me and hear my prayer!
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: have...: or, be gracious unto me

segments.text: O men, how long shall my honor be turned into shame? How long will you love vain words and seek after lies?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: But know that the LORD has set apart the godly for himself; the LORD hears when I call to him.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Be angry, and do not sin; ponder in your own hearts on your beds, and be silent.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Offer right sacrifices, and put your trust in the LORD.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: There are many who say, 'Who will show us some good? Lift up the light of your face upon us, O LORD!'
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: You have put more joy in my heart than they have when their grain and wine abound.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: In peace I will both lie down and sleep; for you alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.
segments.speaker: David
